name: TA-65 Metabolic Results Repair

scope.question: Does PMID 29546832 resolve the NCT02531334 TA-65 metabolic-syndrome trial-watch item, and what public repair should be staged?

summary: Resolved the prior NCT02531334 publication-match question. ClinicalTrials.gov still lists the TA-65 metabolic-syndrome trial as completed with actual December 2017 primary completion, actual June 2018 study completion, and no posted registry results in the June 15, 2026 API view. PubMed and full-text checks identify PMID 29546832 as the result-bearing publication: the article title matches the registry topic, the design matches the randomized double-blind crossover registry, and the full text explicitly states ClinicalTrials.gov protocol NCT02531334 and University of Connecticut IRB protocol H14-278. Staged a surveillance candidate bundle that adds the publication source, adds a disease-context cardiometabolic biomarker finding, reframes the existing no-results finding as no posted registry table, moves the study watch status to results_captured, and refreshes the Telomerase Restoration outlook without changing evidence stage, momentum, or confidence.

materiality_decision.rationale: The finding changes public evidence interpretation enough to require a candidate bundle and outlook support-map refresh, but it is a small disease-context biomarker result and does not change the track rating or require a new coverage assessment.

search_log.summary: A scoped Telomerase Restoration audit identified NCT02531334 as an old late_no_results record with a likely publication match that should be resolved before any retirement decision.

The registry lists NCT02531334 as completed, sponsored by University of Connecticut, with org study ID H14-278, estimated enrollment 40, randomized double-masked crossover design, TA-65/placebo 12-week periods separated by a 3-week washout, actual primary completion in December 2017, actual study completion in June 2018, and hasResults=false.

search_log.query: NCT02531334; "Effects of TA-65, a Telomerase Activator on Metabolic Syndrome"; "TA-65" "metabolic syndrome" Fernandez
search_log.summary: Exact NCT-ID search returned no PubMed records, but exact title/intervention searches returned PMID 29546832. PubMed identifies the article as a 2018 randomized controlled trial in Current Pharmaceutical Design with DOI 10.2174/1381612824666180316114832.

The full text states that the study was approved by the University of Connecticut IRB under protocol H14-278 and registered at ClinicalTrials.gov under protocol NCT02531334. It reports a randomized crossover TA-65/placebo study in 40 recruited metabolic-syndrome participants, with 37 completing the full intervention.
The thesis corroborates the protocol linkage by identifying NCT02531334 and citing the Fernandez 2018 publication, but it was treated as contextual support rather than the primary result source.

Reviewed the live NCT02531334 source, TA-65 metabolic-syndrome study, no-results finding, and Telomerase Restoration outlook. The staged bundle updates those records and adds PMID 29546832 without publishing directly.

trial_watch_checks.summary: Registry status and registry result module are unchanged, but a matched result publication was confirmed through full-text NCT02531334/H14-278 linkage. The study should move to results_captured while preserving no-posted-registry-table caveats.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of a Telomerase Activator on Cardiovascular Risk Factors in Patients with Metabolic Syndrome
excluded_sources.reason: Useful for corroborating the NCT02531334/PMID 29546832 linkage, but the candidate bundle uses the peer-reviewed article as the result-bearing source.

notes: Treat NCT02531334 as no posted registry table, not no results.
Treat PMID 29546832 as disease-context human biomarker evidence with low confidence for LEV relevance.
No rating change is warranted because the result does not demonstrate durable human function, disease-event, mortality, or long-term safety benefit.
next_actions: Run required evidence-review lanes for the submitted candidate bundle.
If approved and published, rerun the global trial-watch audit so NCT02531334 leaves the late no-results retirement-candidate list.
After this repair, sweep remaining old TA-65 late no-results records for possible retirement decisions.
